name: EntryReader

members.name: EntryReader
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Create a EntryReader that uses the given character set.
 * @throws UnsupportedEncodingException if the charset encoding is not supported
 * @param in source from which to read entries
 * @param charsetName the character set to use
 * @param filename non-null file name for stream being read
 * @param comment_re_string regular expression that matches comments.
 *                      Any text that matches comment_re is removed.
 *                      A line that is entirely a comment is ignored.
 * @param include_re_string regular expression that matches include directives.
 *                      The expression should define one group that contains
 *                      the include file name.
 * @see #EntryReader(InputStream,String,String,String)
 */

tags.name: UnsupportedEncodingException
tags.content: if the charset encoding is not supported
tags.description: <code> charset encoding</code> is not supported

tags.name: in
tags.content: source from which to read entries
tags.description: source from which to read entries

tags.name: charsetName
tags.content: the character set to use
tags.description: the character set to use

tags.name: filename
tags.content: non-null file name for stream being read
tags.description: non-null file name for stream being read

tags.name: comment_re_string
tags.content:
regular expression that matches comments.
                     Any text that matches comment_re is removed.
                     A line that is entirely a comment is ignored.

regular expression that matches comments.

                     A line that is entirely a comment is ignored.

/**
 * Create an EntryReader.
 *
 *    @param file       initial file to read
 *    @param comment_re regular expression that matches comments.
 *                      Any text that matches comment_re is removed.
 *                      A line that is entirely a comment is ignored.
 *    @param include_re regular expression that matches include directives.
 *                      The expression should define one group that contains
 *                      the include file name.
 * @throws IOException if there is a problem reading the file
 */

tags.name: file
tags.content: initial file to read
tags.description: the file must be a valid File

regular expression that matches comments.
                     Any text that matches comment_re is removed.
                     A line that is entirely a comment is ignored.
tags.description: comment_re must be a valid regular expression that matches comments.

tags.name: include_re
tags.content:
regular expression that matches include directives.
                     The expression should define one group that contains
                     the include file name.
tags.description: include_re must be a valid regular expression that defines one group containing the include file name.

tags.name: IOException
tags.content: if there is a problem reading the file
tags.description: if there is a problem reading the file

members.name: EntryReader
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Create a new EntryReader starting with the specified file.
 *
 *    @param filename   initial file to read
 *    @param comment_re regular expression that matches comments.
 *                      Any text that matches comment_re is removed.
 *                      A line that is entirely a comment is ignored.
 *    @param include_re regular expression that matches include directives.
 *                      The expression should define one group that contains
 *                      the include file name.
 *
 * @throws IOException if there is a problem reading the file
 *
 * @see #EntryReader(File,String,String)
 */

tags.name: filename
tags.content: initial file to read
tags.assertion: assert receiverObjectID.filename instanceof java.lang.String && receiverObjectID.filename != null;
tags.description: initial file to read must be a String

tags.name: comment_re
tags.content:
regular expression that matches comments.
                     Any text that matches comment_re is removed.
                     A line that is entirely a comment is ignored.
tags.assertion: assert receiverObjectID.comment_re instanceof java.util.regex.Pattern && receiverObjectID.comment_re != null;
tags.description: regular expression that matches comments. Any text that matches comment_re is removed. A line that is entirely a comment is ignored.

tags.name: include_re
tags.content:
regular expression that matches include directives.
                     The expression should define one group that contains
                     the include file name.
tags.assertion: assert receiverObjectID.include_re instanceof java.util.regex.Pattern && receiverObjectID.include_re != null;
tags.description: regular expression that matches include directives. The expression should define one group that contains the include file name.

tags.name: IOException
tags.content: if there is a problem reading the file
tags.description: if there is a problem reading the file

tags.content: #EntryReader(File,String,String)

members.name: readLine
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Read a line, ignoring comments and processing includes.  Note that
 * a line that is completely a comment is completely ignored (and
 * not returned as a blank line).  Returns null at end of file.
 * @return the string that was read, or null at end of file
 */

tags.name: the
tags.content: string that was read, or null at end of file
tags.description: string that was read, or null at end of file

members.name: iterator
members.javadoc:
/** * Return a line-by-line iterator for this file. *

* * Warning: This does not return a fresh iterator each time. The * iterator is a singleton, the same one is returned each time, and a new * one can never be created after it is exhausted. * * @return a line-by-line iterator for this file */

tags.name: a
tags.content: line-by-line iterator for this file

members.name: hasNext
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns whether or not there is another line to read.  Any IOExceptions
 * are turned into errors (because the definition of hasNext() in Iterator
 * doesn't throw any exceptions).
 * @return whether there is another line to read
 */

tags.name: whether
tags.content: there is another line to read

members.name: next
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Return the next line in the multi-file.
 * @return the next line in the multi-file
 * Throws NoSuchElementException at end of file
 */

tags.name: the
tags.content: next line in the multi-file Throws NoSuchElementException at end of file

get_entry
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the next entry (paragraph) in the file.  Entries are separated
 * by blank lines unless the entry started with {@link #entry_start_re}
 * (see {@link #set_entry_start_stop}).  If no more entries are
 * available, returns null.
 * @return the next entry (paragraph) in the file
 * @throws IOException if there is a problem reading the file
 */

tags.name: the
tags.content: next entry (paragraph) in the file

tags.name: IOException
tags.content: if there is a problem reading the file
tags.description: if there is a problem reading the file

members.name: set_entry_start_stop
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Set the regular expressions for the start and stop of long
 * entries (multiple lines that are read as a group by get_entry()).
 * @param entry_start_re regular expression that starts a long entry
 * @param entry_stop_re regular expression that ends a long entry
 */

tags.name: entry_start_re
tags.content: regular expression that starts a long entry
tags.description: entry_start_re must be a String

tags.name: entry_stop_re
tags.content: regular expression that ends a long entry
tags.description: entry_stop_re must be a String

members.name: set_entry_start_stop
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Set the regular expressions for the start and stop of long
 * entries (multiple lines that are read as a group by get_entry()).
 * @param entry_start_re regular expression that starts a long entry
 * @param entry_stop_re regular expression that ends a long entry
 */

tags.name: entry_start_re
tags.content: regular expression that starts a long entry
tags.assertion: assert receiverObjectID.entry_start_re != null && receiverObjectID.entry_start_re instanceof java.util.regex.Pattern;
tags.description: the regular expression that starts a long entry must be valid

tags.name: entry_stop_re
tags.content: regular expression that ends a long entry
tags.assertion: assert receiverObjectID.entry_stop_re != null && receiverObjectID.entry_stop_re instanceof java.util.regex.Pattern;
tags.description: the regular expression that ends a long entry must be valid
